intro: Modern study methods leverage cognitive science: spaced repetition, active recall, and interleaving. Applied correctly, you learn faster with greater retention. This calculator estimates how many hours you need to reach 80% mastery in any skill. Free and fast. Values are updated for 2026 and reviewed regularly to keep you current.

explanation:
## Understanding Learning Curves and the 80/20 Principle

Modern study methods and learning techniques are grounded in cognitive science (Ebbinghaus, Bjork, Dunlosky). Techniques like spaced repetition, active recall, and interleaving have solid evidence backing them.

## How It's Calculated

This calculator applies the learning curve formula to estimate how many hours you need to reach 80% mastery in a skill based on your inputs. It verifies typical ranges and alerts you if values fall outside reasonable bounds.

## Final Notes

This calculation is an estimate for reference. For critical decisions (financial, medical, legal), consult a specialized professional. Values are updated for 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.a: The 80/20 principle states that 20% of effort typically yields 80% of results. In learning, initial progress is fast, but reaching advanced mastery takes more time and effort.

faq.q: How many hours does it take to master a skill?
faq.a: Time varies by skill complexity and your current level. This calculator uses learning curve research to provide a realistic estimate based on your inputs.
The calculator applies the learning curve formula based on cognitive science principles (spaced repetition, active recall, interleaving) validated internationally.
Results are estimates. We use formulas validated internationally. For critical decisions, consult a professional. This is a good starting point for planning your learning path.
Techniques are validated in academic publications based on cognitive science research from Ebbinghaus, Bjork, and Dunlosky.
Yes, all our calculators are free with no registration required.

faq.q: Do you store my personal data?
faq.a: No. All calculations happen in your browser. We don't send data to any server.
References are updated for 2026 and reviewed regularly to ensure accuracy.

faq.q: Can I use this for any skill?
faq.a: Yes, the 80/20 learning principle applies universally. Adjust your inputs based on skill complexity and your available learning time.
